problem_statement: It examines how open source software projects govern GenAI-generated contributions (PRs, issues, code reviews, security reports) whose cheap generation outpaces maintainers' capacity to review, verify, and maintain them.
magnitude_evidence: When a Node.js TSC member submitted a 19,000-line PR generated with Claude Code, the incident triggered a petition by over 80 developers calling for a project-wide ban on AI-assisted contributions; corpus drawn from 67 highly visible OSS projects.
insufficiency_evidence: "surface completeness no longer signals correctness" — Metasploit submissions were "well-formatted, looked really neat, and did nothing it said it did"; "review is the bottleneck. Not code generation"; low-effort AI submissions described as "a Denial-of-service attack on our human effort"; text-based rules are "often reactive and might easily bypassed by coding agents or low-effort human submitters".
solution_mechanisms: transparency and disclosure of AI use
verification and evidence gating (test logs, PoC)
notes: Focus is OSS community/policy governance of AI contributions rather than a technical oversight tool; formal regulatory frameworks (EU AI Act, NIST) are not cited.
